ID:       ddx-5d49b14e
Title:    Evaluate and adopt a real backend for the bead tracker (axon primary, bd fallback)
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:strategy, backend-migration
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-b18d9ce6
Desc:     The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).
Accept:   1. TD authored for the axon-backend integration (data model mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, multi-machine concurrency story). 2. Axon-backend implementation lands behind a feature flag or selectable backend in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. 3. Migration tool moves an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl into the axon backend without data loss. 4. Existing chaos_test.go suite passes against the axon backend. 5. bd fallback documented (single artifact under docs/) showing how to wire bd as the backend if axon adoption stalls. 6. Performance: queue ops (ready/blocked/show) at parity or better than JSONL on the current 1100-bead archive scale.
Created:  2026-05-02 13:06:50
Updated:  2026-05-02 14:44:55
Claimed:  2026-05-02T14:44:55Z
Machine:  eitri
execute-loop-heartbeat-at: 2026-05-02T14:44:55.123887274Z

data.text: This bead is a classic epic-too-big — labeled `kind:strategy`, typed `epic`, with 6 AC spanning design, implementation, migration tool, chaos tests, fallback docs, and benchmarks. Description even says "Children break down the work." Decomposing.

input.command:
ddx bead create "TD: axon backend integration design for bead tracker" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:design, backend-migration" \
  --description "Author a Technical Design (TD) document for replacing the JSONL+flock+git bead backend with an axon-backed implementation. Cover: (a) data model mapping — how Bead structs and the events log map onto axon collections/streams; (b) claim/lock semantics — how 'claim a ready bead' becomes an axon op, including multi-worker fairness and cross-machine concurrency (Story 14 federation); (c) archive policy — how closed-bead externalization (current .ddx/attachments + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl per ADR-004) carries over; (d) JSONL interchange — keep bd/br import/export compatibility per FEAT-004; (e) failure modes and recovery; (f) migration story (forward refs to migration-tool child). Cite ADR-004/SD-004 and ddx-da11a34a (current lock-contention patch). Output the TD under docs/helix/04-design/td-axon-bead-backend.md (or appropriate path). No code changes." \
  --acceptance "1. TD document committed under docs/. 2. Covers data model, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, JSONL interchange, failure modes, and migration story. 3. Cites ADR-004/SD-004 and ddx-da11a34a. 4. Identifies open questions and decisions needed before implementation."

input.command:
ddx bead create "Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag in cli/internal/bead" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:implementation, backend-migration" \
  --description "Implement the axon-backed Backend interface in cli/internal/bead/backend.go, selectable at runtime via config flag (e.g., backend: jsonl|axon in .ddx/config.yaml). Must satisfy the same interface JSONL backend currently satisfies: create/show/update/close/list/ready/blocked/dep ops, plus claim semantics and event append. Follows the TD authored in the design child. Existing JSONL path remains the default until migration is proven. No data migration in this child — that's a separate bead." \
  --acceptance "1. Axon Backend implementation lives in cli/internal/bead/ alongside the JSONL backend. 2. Selectable via config; JSONL remains default. 3. All Backend interface methods implemented. 4. Unit tests cover create/claim/close/dep on the axon backend. 5. ddx bead commands work against the axon backend on a fresh repo (no migration required)."

input.command:
ddx bead create "Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:migration, backend-migration" \
  --description "Build a one-shot migration command (e.g., ddx bead migrate --to axon) that reads an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl, plus per-bead .ddx/attachments/, and loads them losslessly into the axon backend. Idempotent and safe to re-run. Includes a verification pass: round-trip count, claim-state preservation, event-history preservation. Depends on the axon backend implementation child." \
  --acceptance "1. Migration command implemented and wired into the CLI. 2. Migrates beads.jsonl + beads-archive.jsonl + attachments without data loss. 3. Idempotent (re-running is a no-op). 4. Verification step compares pre/post counts and event totals. 5. Integration test runs migration on a fixture repo and asserts equivalence."

input.content:
Bead ddx-5d49b14e is an epic (kind:strategy, type:epic). Its description
explicitly says "Children break down the work" and the AC list spans six
unrelated subsystem deliverables: design doc, implementation, migration tool,
chaos tests, fallback documentation, and a performance benchmark. This is too
large for a single bounded execute-bead attempt per the size-check guidance.

Decomposed into six child beads (parent=ddx-5d49b14e), one per AC:

  ddx-3b06699f  TD: axon backend integration design for bead tracker     (AC1)
  ddx-c9ef6c4f  Implement axon backend behind selectable backend flag    (AC2)
  ddx-57c4cd99  Migration tool: beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend     (AC3)
  ddx-dfb1c6d7  chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend          (AC4)
  ddx-0c8c9670  Document bd/doltdb fallback wiring for bead backend      (AC5)
  ddx-781a15cf  Benchmark: axon backend vs JSONL on 1100-bead archive    (AC6)

Dependency edges recorded:
  ddx-c9ef6c4f depends on ddx-3b06699f   (impl after design)
  ddx-57c4cd99 depends on ddx-c9ef6c4f   (migration after impl)
  ddx-dfb1c6d7 depends on ddx-c9ef6c4f   (chaos tests after impl)
  ddx-781a15cf depends on ddx-c9ef6c4f   (benchmark after impl)
  ddx-0c8c9670 has no deps               (fallback docs are independent)

Workaround note: `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worktree
mis-derived the bead ID prefix from the worktree directory basename
(e.g. ".execute-bead-wt-ddx-5d49b14e-...-<hex>") because
`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` from a linked worktree returns the worktree
path, not the project root. Setting DDX_BEAD_PREFIX=ddx as an env override
produced correct ddx-* IDs. Four malformed-ID beads created during the
diagnosis pass were closed. detectPrefix() in cli/internal/bead/store.go
already has the intent of running git from the project root, but the
caller-provided workingDir in this invocation path resolves to the worktree;
worth a follow-up bead, but out of scope for this decomposition attempt.

No source changes in this commit; only the per-attempt evidence file.

Decomposed `ddx-5d49b14e` into six child beads with dependency edges. Rationale file committed under `.ddx/executions/20260502T144455-ed29df8c/no_changes_rationale.txt`.

Children:
- `ddx-3b06699f` — TD design doc (AC1)
- `ddx-c9ef6c4f` — axon backend impl (AC2, deps: 3b06699f)
- `ddx-57c4cd99` — migration tool (AC3, deps: c9ef6c4f)
- `ddx-dfb1c6d7` — chaos tests (AC4, deps: c9ef6c4f)
- `ddx-0c8c9670` — bd fallback docs (AC5, independent)
- `ddx-781a15cf` — benchmark (AC6, deps: c9ef6c4f)

Side note: hit a `ddx bead create` prefix-detection bug inside execute-bead worktrees (worked around with `DDX_BEAD_PREFIX=ddx`); flagged in the rationale as a possible follow-up.
